strengths: 20 years of continuous software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — comfortably clears the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform actively used by NGOs, demonstrating builder instinct and product ownership
Recent hands-on work with MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq shows early but real engagement with AI infrastructure tooling
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and very recent — limited to ~14 months at re:cinq; no demonstrated AI products shipped at scale, no publications, and no recognized standing in the AI field
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field; dual bachelor's degrees are unrelated to ML or AI, leaving the preferred academic signal entirely absent

Profile is that of a hands-on software craftsman, not a director-level technical strategist — no evidence of advising executive customers, influencing product roadmaps at the organizational level, or shaping AI adoption strategy
response.justification: The role's central requirement is recognized AI expertise with shipped AI products or platforms — the candidate's only AI-related work is 14 months of agentic tooling at re:cinq, with no prior AI background, no publications, and no demonstrated influence in the field. The 20-year engineering career satisfies the tenure floor, and the GISCollective founder role shows entrepreneurial instinct, but the entire career reads as a hands-on individual contributor focused on software craftsmanship rather than strategic technical leadership. There is no evidence of advising enterprise customers on AI adoption, influencing cloud product direction, or operating at the director-level scope this position requires. The gap between the candidate's current ceiling and the role's floor is too wide to bridge in an interview process.
